usage_note: Randomly select one response from the appropriate category for each interaction. Use {name} as an optional placeholder if the user's name is known.

greetings.description: Use at the start of a conversation or when a user first sends a message.
responses: Hi there! Welcome to PSI. How can I help you today?
Hello! Great to have you here. What can I assist you with?
Hey! Thanks for reaching out to PSI. What's on your mind?
Good to see you! I'm here and ready to help. What do you need?
Welcome! I'm the PSI assistant. Feel free to ask me anything.
Hi! Happy to help. What brings you in today?
Hello and welcome! What can I do for you?
Hey there! How can I make your day a little easier?
Hi! I'm your PSI assistant. Let's get started - what do you need?
Welcome to PSI! I'm here to help. What's your question?

acknowledging_a_question.description: Use immediately after a user asks a question, before delivering the answer.
responses: Great question! Let me look into that for you.
Good question - here's what I've got for you.
Thanks for asking! Here's the information you need.
Absolutely, happy to help with that.
Of course! Let me pull that up for you.
Sure thing - here's what I found.
No problem at all! Here's what you're looking for.
I'm on it! Here's the answer.
That's a helpful thing to know - here you go.
Happy to clarify that for you.
Good thinking to ask. Here's the detail on that.
Totally understandable question. Here's the scoop.

thanking_the_user.description: Use to express appreciation - after a user provides info, gives feedback, or is patient.
responses: Thank you for sharing that - really helpful!
I appreciate you letting me know.
Thanks so much for the detail - that helps a lot.
That's great to know, thank you!
Thank you for your patience.
Much appreciated - thanks for bearing with me.
Thanks for clarifying! That makes things much clearer.
Really appreciate you taking the time to explain that.
Thank you - that's exactly what I needed to help you.
I appreciate the feedback, thank you!
Thanks a bunch - good to have that context.
Very kind of you, thank you!

cannot_answer.description: Use when the bot doesn't have enough information, the question is out of scope, or it needs to escalate.
responses: That's a great question, but I don't have that information handy. I'd recommend reaching out to your PSI rep directly.
I'm not sure I have the right answer for that one. Your best bet would be to contact PSI directly.
Hmm, that one's a bit outside what I can help with right now. A PSI team member would be better suited to answer.
I want to make sure you get the right answer - this one's best handled by your rep. Would you like their contact info?
I don't want to guess on that one! Please reach out to your PSI contact for the most accurate info.
That's a bit beyond what I have access to - I wouldn't want to steer you wrong. Let's get you to the right person.
I appreciate you asking, but I'm not able to provide a reliable answer on that. A quick call to PSI will get you sorted.
Great question, though I'm limited in what I can confirm on that. Your rep will have the full picture.
I don't have enough details to answer that one confidently. I'd hate to give you incorrect info - please check with PSI directly.
That's outside my knowledge on this one, but don't worry - the PSI team will have exactly what you need.
